records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Breathed (brēd); p. pr. & vb. n. Breathing.]

defs: To respire; to inhale and exhale air; hence;, to live.
quotes: Breathes there a man with soul so dead Who never to himself hath said, This is my own, my native land!

defs: To take breath; to rest from action.
quotes: Well! breathe awhile, and then to it again!

defs: To pass like breath; noiselessly or gently; to exhale; to emanate; to blow gently.
quotes: The air breathes upon us here most sweetly.
There breathes a living fragrance from the shore.

defs: To inhale and exhale in the process of respiration; to respire.
quotes: To view the light of heaven, and breathe the vital air.

defs: To inject by breathing; to infuse; -- with into.
quotes: Able to breathe life into a stone.
And the Lord God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life.

defs: To emit or utter by the breath; to utter softly; to whisper; as, to breathe a vow.
quotes: Or let the church, our mother, breathe her curse, A mother's curse, on her revolting son.

defs: To exhale; to emit, as breath; as, the flowers breathe odors or perfumes.
To express; to manifest; to give forth.
quotes: Others articles breathe the same severe spirit.

defs: To act upon by the breath; to cause to sound by breathing.
To promote free respiration in; to exercise.
quotes: And every man should beat thee. I think thou wast created for men to breathe themselves upon thee.

defs: To suffer to take breath, or recover the natural breathing; to rest; as, to breathe a horse.
quotes: A moment breathed his panting steed.

defs: To put out of breath; to exhaust.
quotes: Mr. Tulkinghorn arrives in his turret room, a little breathed by the journey up.

defs: To utter without vocality, as the nonvocal consonants.
quotes: The same sound may be pronounces either breathed, voiced, or whispered.
Breathed elements, being already voiceless, remain unchanged [in whispering].
